claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements JSON drift repair with explicit fail-closed handling for unrecoverable JSON

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate function implements repair patterns for Claude JSON drift: line range format, empty key names, and trailing commas
evidence.why: This is the core logic that handles recoverable JSON formatting issues from Claude agents

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject function attempts JSON extraction from direct text, fenced code blocks, and slice-by-braces fallback
evidence.why: Shows the multi-strategy approach to finding valid JSON in various agent output formats

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult function returns explicit EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code when parsed is null, with missing array containing the error message
evidence.why: This implements the fail-closed requirement for unrecoverable JSON with explicit blocker codes

evidence.summary: Provider run result includes json_repaired boolean flag that tracks whether drift repair was applied
evidence.why: This is the recording mechanism for when minor drift is successfully repaired

evidence.summary: Test 'repairs minor Claude JSON key drift and records the repair' validates line format and empty key drift patterns are repaired and json_repaired flag is set
evidence.why: This test confirms the repair logic works for documented drift patterns

evidence.summary: Test 'invalid agent JSON fails closed with explicit blocker code' validates that non-JSON input returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON and keeps readiness blocked
evidence.why: This test confirms the fail-closed behavior for unrecoverable JSON

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines required fields and contract for evidence agent results including error handling expectations
evidence.why: This defines the schema contract that agents must follow and what blockers are required

evidence.summary: Demand doctrine specifies that dispatch returns are fed back into PRD readiness, and any violation keeps blockers active
evidence.why: This is the product-level requirement that validates the implementation approach

assumptions: The implementation has not been stress-tested with real Claude -p agents in production
Dirty working tree mentioned in status_quo may indicate development state but no evidence of pending changes
risks: If Claude introduces new JSON drift patterns beyond the three handled cases, agents may fail with EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON even for recoverable issues
The repair logic is regex-based and may not handle all edge cases of malformed JSON
Test coverage may not include all possible Claude output variations

result.notes: Evidence confirms implementation exists with test coverage for JSON drift repair and fail-closed handling. The code structure matches the demand requirements: recoverable drift is repaired and recorded, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness remains blocked until valid evidence is provided.
